title: Negotiate capabilities before invoking server features
description: Advertise and check the capabilities each side declared during initialization, and degrade gracefully instead of calling features the peer never offered.
body: Use when a host connects to an MCP server or a server exposes optional features (tools, resources, prompts, sampling). Do not assume a peer supports a method because your version does, and avoid calling resource or prompt endpoints a server never advertised. Actions: complete the initialize handshake, read the declared capabilities object, and gate each call on the corresponding advertised capability; record which features were negotiated. Validation signals include an initialize exchange in the trace showing matching capabilities and a graceful 'method not supported' path test. Risk boundary: if a required capability is absent, surface a clear unsupported-feature error rather than crashing or silently no-oping, and escalate version mismatches.
